attributes.content:
## Adjust the Mortgage Calculator card

`sourceCardUrl` above points at the Mortgage Calculator card in the catalog.

## The changes

Two things:

1. **Add an extra-monthly-payment input, and show its impact on the loan.** Borrowers constantly ask: *what if I pay a bit extra toward the principal each month?* The calculator can't answer that today.

2. **Restyle it to look like a 90s-era corporate insurance app.** Think the beige, boxy, all-business desktop software an insurance office ran back then — chunky bordered panels, system fonts, dense form layout, that kind of vibe. Lean into it.

Work the details out yourself from how the card already behaves — it already computes the full amortization (loan amount, monthly payment, lifetime interest, and so on). Decide what fields, math, and presentation make sense, and keep everything the card already does working.

## Done when

Someone can enter an extra monthly payment and see how it changes the payoff, and the calculator has that 90s corporate-insurance look.

cardInfo.name: Adjust Mortgage Calculator: extra-payment input + 90s insurance-app restyle
cardInfo.summary: Add an extra-monthly-payment input to the catalog Mortgage Calculator and restyle it as a 90s-era corporate insurance app — details left to the implementer.
